content:
🚀 whosthere | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Discovering and visualizing devices on a local area network through an interactive terminal interface.
✨ Key Features: • Interactive TUI • Fast & Concurrent scanning • No Elevated Privileges Required • Device Enrichment (OUI lookup) • Integrated Port Scanner
📖 Summary: Whosthere is a Go-based Local Area Network discovery tool that uses an interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) to help users understand their network. It employs multiple unprivileged scanning techniques like mDNS, SSDP, and ARP cache analysis, enriching discovered devices with manufacturer information (OUI lookups).
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 homehub | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a centralized, private, and easy-to-use dashboard for families to manage various daily utilities and information on their home network.
✨ Key Features: • Shared Notes • Shared Cloud (File Upload/Sharing) • Shopping List with history suggestions • Chore Tracker • Calendar & Reminders
📖 Summary: HomeHub is a private, self-hosted, and lightweight web application designed to centralize family utilities. It offers a no-login, Progressive Web App (PWA) experience for managing shared notes, shopping lists, chores, media downloads, and more, all within your home network.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 cyberpanel | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Web hosting management and server administration.
✨ Key Features: • Auto SSL • FTP Server • Light-weight DNS Server (PowerDNS) • phpMyAdmin • Email Support (SnappyMail)
📖 Summary: CyberPanel is a web hosting control panel powered by OpenLiteSpeed, designed to simplify server and website management. It offers a wide range of features for managing domains, emails, databases, security, and PHP versions, making it a comprehensive solution for hosting providers and developers.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 openlitespeed | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Serving web content efficiently and at high speeds.
✨ Key Features: • High-performance HTTP server • Lightweight • Open source • GPLv3 license • Developed by LiteSpeed Technologies
📖 Summary: OpenLiteSpeed is a high-performance, lightweight, open-source HTTP server developed by LiteSpeed Technologies. It is distributed under the GPLv3 license and offers robust web serving capabilities with a focus on speed and efficiency.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 nightingale | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a powerful and flexible alerting engine for monitoring systems, processing and distributing alarms effectively, and integrating with existing data sources and visualization tools.
✨ Key Features: • Open-source alerting expert • Connects with various data sources (VictoriaMetrics, ElasticSearch, etc.) • Configurable alerting and notification rules
📖 Summary: Nightingale is an open-source alerting system designed to work with existing data sources, focusing on processing and distributing alarms. It complements visualization tools like Grafana by providing a robust alerting engine and supports distributed deployments for resilience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
